title: Save the Date – My Sporting Heritage Final Conference, 12 September 2026, Zagreb
content:
Save the Date – My Sporting Heritage Final Conference, 12 September 2026, Zagreb

After nearly three years of research, collaboration, and discovery, the My Sporting Heritage project is reaching its final step. The virtual exhibition – created by MCA to explore stories and images that bring to life the events, places, and people who shaped the history of European sport – can be visited at https://my-sporting-heritage.eu/virtual-exhibition.  

Join us for the Final Conference of the My Sporting Heritage project, where partners, researchers, heritage professionals, educators, and sport enthusiasts from across Europe will come together to celebrate and discuss the project’s achievements and future perspectives.

Funded by the Erasmus+ Sport programme, My Sporting Heritage aims to identify, preserve, and promote Europe’s rich yet often overlooked sporting heritage, while strengthening a sense of European belonging through sport, culture, and shared values. Throughout the project, partners have developed research activities, educational resources, digital tools, and a virtual exhibition to make Europe’s sporting heritage more accessible to younger generations and the wider public.

title: Inside the Digital Heritage Summit 2026!
content:
We participated in the Digital Heritage Summit 2026, held in Limassol, Cyprus, from 26–29 May 2026. The summit brought together cultural heritage professionals, researchers, policymakers and technology experts to discuss the future of digital heritage, innovation, and preservation across Europe.

As a cooperating partner of the event, we contributed to discussions on digital transformation, cultural heritage data, and European cooperation in the sector. Our involvement reflects our ongoing mission to support the preservation, promotion and reuse of digital cultural heritage through networking, capacity building, advocacy and participation in major European initiatives.

Our General Coordinator, Corinne Szteinsznaider, represented us at the opening session of a high-level working day dedicated to strengthening European cooperation against the illicit trafficking of cultural goods. Our Policy Manager, Marco Fiore, presented the policy paper developed within the ANCHISE project, addressing key challenges and recommendations related to combating the illicit trafficking of cultural heritage.
